The leadership’s unwillingness to engage is disappointing and only hurts the Palestinian people that they claim to serve.” [**The Trump Administration is Ready to Help California Meet Wildfire Threat**](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTA4Ljk0NjM1NDUxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkwOC45NDYzNTQ1MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzgzNjg2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&108&&&https://www.sacbee.com/opinion/op-ed/soapbox/article217795565.html) _-The Sacramento Bee_ Secretary of the Interior Ryan Zinke and Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue write that the day the Carr Fire started, FEMA approved a grant to help California with firefighting costs. "There is no denying that fires have been a part of Earth’s history since forests first flourished on the planet," they write. "What we can control is how we prepare and respond to them."

subject: Never quit
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3725/9e00690/5bd58b38/2402aa63/1092852907/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx&source=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx")
Friends,

I had the honor of competing with Mayor Andrew Gillum for the last 18 months, and this is what I learned: Andrew never, ever gives up.

He never quits -- so I know he'll never quit on you. And this is why I am incredibly honored and humbled to join him on the Democratic ticket this fall as his Lieutenant Governor.

**[It's time for a progressive movement that will move our state forward, and Andrew and I are ready to make that happen. But we need you standing with us -- will you chip in $5, or whatever you can afford, right now?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3725/9e00690/5bd58b38/2402aa62/1092852907/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-lg?gpsid=13_55_3445&refcode=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=2+-+Its+time+for+a+progressive+movement+that")**

Like my wife Kristen and I, the Gillums have two sons and one daughter, but that's not the only thing we have in common -- our families are deeply committed to our communities and the future of our state.

Andrew and I share a passion for education and improving Florida's workforce to give people the opportunity to succeed in today's economy. We will stand up to the divisive politics of Ron DeSantis and Donald Trump, and offer Floridians a vision that will lift us up. Together, Andrew and I will unite this state.

**[I'm committed to fighting alongside Andrew and helping him rebuild our state and economy into one that works for every Floridian -- are you with us? Chip in $5, or whatever you can afford, to help us bring it home this November.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3725/9e00690/5bd58b38/2402aa65/1092852907/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-lg?gpsid=13_55_3445&refcode=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180907_LGpst2_fnd_gq_act__CK_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=3+-+Im+committed+to+fighting+alongside+Andre")**

I'm excited to see what we're going to accomplish together.

Thank you,

Chris King

subject: re: Pence in town
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY FRIENDS FOR STEVE SISOLAK
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/1K2gnwlvULKs7zNDXhAxX43aukC4XV6iS1b_plkATmQ=/VCLG44ZgzlqA97HTc0UKIn6qQ0-AcH8jlxBGv2l_Xto=?gpsid=37_57_3453&refcode=em18_180908_pence2_LG_fnd_gq_act__MK&utm_campaign=em18_180908_pence2_LG_fnd_gq_act__MK&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ssiso)
---

Well, Mike Pence was here yesterday campaigning for his buddy Adam Laxalt.

Money poured in as wealthy donors ponied up for the chance to take photos with the Vice President and bend Lacky Laxalt's ears about their pet issues.

**[We can't afford to fall behind to this influx of massive checks to Adam Laxalt's campaign. Will you help us keep up by chipping in whatever you can?](https://act.myngp.com/el/1K2gnwlvULKs7zNDXhAxX43aukC4XV6iS1b_plkATmQ=/VCLG44ZgzlqA97HTc0UKIn6qQ0-AcH8jlxBGv2l_Xto=?gpsid=37_57_3453&refcode=em18_180908_pence2_L1_fnd_gq_act__MK&utm_campaign=em18_180908_pence2_L1_fnd_gq_act__MK&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ssiso)**

name: Josh Hawley
subject: Kavanaugh and our country deserve better
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate
body:
With so much at stake - religious liberty, immigration, the Second Amendment - our country needs someone like Judge Kavanaugh on the Supreme Court. Hello
Hello Peter,

The hearings for Judge Kavanaugh so far have been a show of liberal Senators at their worst. Democrats have refused to listen during his hearings, choosing theatrics and disruption over doing their jobs.

Kavanaugh should be having one of the smoothest confirmation hearings in Supreme Court history. He is remarkably qualified, fair, and he has an almost unparalleled wealth of experience. Many have said that President Trump could not have made a better choice, and I must say I agree.

With so much at stake - religious liberty, immigration, the Second Amendment - our country needs someone like Judge Kavanaugh on the Supreme Court.

That’s why the grandstanding by those on the left is nothing short of shocking, and it’s why McCaskill’s blatant obstruction must end. Kavanaugh deserves better, but more importantly, our country deserves better.

Many Missourians who voted for Donald Trump did so because he promised to nominate qualified, conservative judges. Well, McCaskill made a promise too. She promised to represent Missouri’s interests in the Senate, a promise she is once again breaking. By choosing not to support this nominee, McCaskill is blatantly putting party leadership before her own state.

She's willfully letting her own constituents down.

This shouldn’t be about party loyalty, it should be about the future of our judicial system. I am running for Senate because in times like these, Missouri doesn’t have a voice with Claire McCaskill in office.

I hope the rest of Judge Kavanaugh’s confirmation hearing is more civil, and that he is quickly confirmed. I know Judge Kavanaugh will be a fantastic Supreme Court justice.

For Missouri,
Josh Hawley

Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate

name: U.S. Senator Bernie Sanders
subject: How does it happen that the richest man in the history of the world is also one of the largest recipients of welfare in America?
body:
[1]Bernie Sanders

How does it happen, sisters and brothers, that the wealthiest man in the
history of the world, Jeff Bezos, is also one of the largest welfare
recipients in America?

It is quite simple, really.

Jeff Bezos and his company, Amazon, make huge profits by paying their
employees wages that are so inadequate that many of them need public
assistance just to get by. Today in America, thousands of Amazon workers
are forced to rely on food stamps, Medicaid and public housing because
they can't survive on the wages they receive. Meanwhile, Jeff Bezos is now
worth $158 billion, and his wealth increases by $260 million - every
single day. How absurd is that?

And who pays for the public assistance subsidizing Mr. Bezos’ wealth? You
do. The middle class subsidizes the wealthiest person in the world, while
his workers struggle to put food on the table. That is what the rigged
economy is all about. And in my view, that has got to end.

That is why I introduced a piece of legislation that aims to end corporate
welfare by establishing a 100 percent tax on corporations with 500 or more
employees equal to the amount of federal benefits received by their
low-wage workers.

So, if a worker at Amazon receives $1,000 in food stamps, Amazon would be
taxed $1,000 to cover that cost.

The bill gives large, profitable employers a choice: pay your workers a
living wage or pay for the public assistance they need to get by. It’s
common sense. Now I want you to send a message to my colleagues that it
has your support, as well:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/BEZOS-Citizen-Co-Sponsor?source=em180907-full&t=1001&akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia ]Add your name as an original Citizen Co-Sponsor of my Stop Bad
Employers by Zeroing Out Subsidies (BEZOS) Act and send a message that
taxpayers should not have to expend huge sums of money subsidizing
profitable corporations owned by some of the wealthiest people in America.

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/BEZOS-Citizen-Co-Sponsor?source=em180907-full&t=1002&akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia ]ADD YOUR NAME

Make no mistake about it, Jeff Bezos is not alone in this regard. Some of
the most profitable businesses in America sustain their wealth through
this kind of corporate welfare.

According to one report, in 2014, Walmart employees received at least $6.2
billion in public aid every year. Walmart is owned by the Walton family,
the wealthiest family in the country.

More than half of employees in the fast food industry rely on some kind of
public assistance.

McDonald’s workers are actually encouraged to sign up for assistance, and
the co-owner of Burger King has a net worth of $25 billion, while his
workers receive an estimated $356 million in subsidies each year.

According to a study from the University of California, low wages cost
American taxpayers $150 billion every single year.

So let me be as clear as I can be: The government has a moral
responsibility to make certain that every man, woman and child in this
country has a decent standard of living and that we provide for the
vulnerable in this country — our kids, the sick, the elderly and the
disabled. It is not acceptable, however, that the American taxpayer is
being asked to subsidize the wealth of some of the wealthiest people in
the history of the world.

And it must end.

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/BEZOS-Citizen-Co-Sponsor?source=em180907-full&t=1003&akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia ]Please add your name as an original Citizen Co-Sponsor of my Stop Bad
Employers by Zeroing Out Subsidies (BEZOS) Act and send a message that
taxpayers should not have to expend huge sums of money subsidizing
profitable corporations owned by some of the wealthiest people in America.

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/BEZOS-Citizen-Co-Sponsor?source=em180907-full&t=1004&akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia ]ADD YOUR NAME

The economic and political systems of this country are stacked against
ordinary Americans and in the favor of the most powerful among us.

The rich get richer and use their wealth to buy elections — just recently,
it was announced that Jeff Bezos made a $10 million donation to a Super
PAC that in one place is running ads supporting a candidate who will “work
with President Trump to fight for Florida’s conservative values.”

For the rest of us, who don’t have the wealth it takes to buy the
legislative outcomes they want, it’s survival of the fittest.

That is not democracy. That is oligarchy. Let's work together to end it.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/BEZOS-Citizen-Co-Sponsor?source=em180907-full&t=1005&akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia ]ADD YOUR NAME

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.berniesanders.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=134%2E150750%2ETj2Aia

subject: Some good news and an ask
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
We can push Zephyr over the finish line.
#

Peter -
We have just 5 days to go before the primary. It's the home stretch, and that means we are working harder than ever to push Zephyr over the finish line.
Here's the good news: The momentum is on our side. We get new endorsements every day, and our grassroots team is growing more and more. We've finalized our plans and our budget, so we know exactly what we have to do to win this thing.
But that's where we need your help: We know what we need to spend to win, but we cannot do it if we don't have the resources to fight back against our opponents' corporate money. So I have to ask:
**[Can you make a $3 donation today to support our campaign in the final stretch? Wall Street and the real estate industry are pouring money in at the last minute to spend against us, but if we're in this together, we can beat them.](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780973/6e822e2f/1d526328/4173a6b0/1136134534/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA5MDgtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-september?refcode=em180908-1-full")**
We're running an aggressive campaign -- reaching out to voters on every platform. From field to digital to TV, we're making sure everyone knows why we need Zephyr as our next Attorney General.
But running this kind of campaign is not cheap, and since we don't take corporate money, we rely on grassroots supporters like you to chip in whatever you can afford to help our effort.
So please, make a donation to help keep up our momentum:
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/5-days](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780973/6e822e2f/1d526328/4173a6b0/1136134534/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA5MDgtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-september?refcode=em180908-1-full")**
Your support means the world to our team. We could not do this without you.
All my best,
Dan Torres

clean_body: There's no doubt in my mind about which option I prefer. Wisconsin made it clear in 2016 that they have had enough of DC politics as usual, too. Friend, in less than 60 days, Wisconsin has the chance to change the fate of our entire country. You might think that sounds like an exaggeration, but friend, it's not. Winning in November could be the difference between a Senate majority that wants to work and get things done for Americans from all walks of life or one that just wants to resist and obstruct. Defeating Tammy Baldwin could be the difference between progress on our America First agenda, and more of the same in Washington — where nothing gets done. There's no doubt in my mind about which option I prefer. Wisconsin made it clear in 2016 that they have had enough of DC politics as usual, too. Our do-nothing senator Tammy Baldwin needs to go! And we've got only two months until Election Day. Friend, in Wisconsin, I’ve led the charge to get more people working than ever before in state history, cut taxes by $8 billion and get unemployment under 3 percent. Now I’m committed to get things done in the U.S. Senate by fighting for the middle class, fixing healthcare and lowering taxes. We can't afford to lose! That’s why I'm reaching out to each and every supporter to make sure we're all doing our part to win this thing. If you can chip in today to help us build our funds in these final months, we'll be one step closer to victory. Please, stand with me today and help keep us going full speed ahead until November. Contribute today: I am counting on you. Thanks, Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: DWS (Congresswoman)
subject: sheer insanity
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Public Education is woefully underfunded. Many times, teachers dip into their own pockets in order to buy basic supplies. body:
Derek, I'm hearing that people are worried. Concerned Virginians are worried about the Supreme Court ---------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hey there,

Thanks to you I got to spend my August going to more than 60 events across Virginia, talking to Virginians.

**Derek, I 'm hearing that people are worried.** Farmers and business owners are worried about tariffs. Concerned Virginians are worried about the Supreme Court - what it could mean for things like women's health care and labor unions. They're worried about families staying together, schools being staffed, and about our president being held accountable.

And I tell you what - having these conversations, hearing these concerns - these are the kind of things that _should_ be taking up my time. Your worries are my worries, your family succeeding is how I measure my success.

I'm going to keep getting out there and meeting folks and hearing their stories as much as I possibly can. I'm going to keep joining our great House candidates in their districts so they can meet their (present _or future!_) constituents, and talk about how we can make this a place that works for all.

**Can you help me keep it up, Derek? When you chip in online, you power the work I do out there - that 's more events and more people I can _really listen to_. I'm betting on you all helping me hit my September Quarter goal to keep this train moving.
